behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MIN-FAILING-INVARIANT: a single rename event must increment only `renamed` and must not also increment `added`/`deleted` for the same file move. This failure boundary is at real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/aggregation, which requires a real repository and real git command behavior (rename heuristics/thresholds and status formats). MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: canned parser fixtures can pass while real git output (or rename detection toggling) breaks classification. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/repo state is involved, so Integration is too low. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text being purely git-diff categorization with no AI/model decision or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit file `a.txt`, then rename to `b.txt` and commit (or diff target window used by tool). Run the CLI against that repo via real subprocess. Assert report has `renamed=1` and does not count that rename as both `added` and `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MIN-FAILING-INVARIANT: when at least one changed file is binary, CLI completes successfully and includes that file in counts. Real binary diff handling depends on actual git output/bytes and subprocess I/O; decode/parsing crashes often appear only with real binary patches. MOCK-HIDE: text fixtures can miss encoding/stream edge cases from real git. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess and filesystem artifacts are required. REJECTION-GATE: no AI-quality or multi-step agent behavior is described, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit an initial binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, then run CLI over the change range through real git. Assert non-error exit and that binary file contributes to category totals.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MIN-FAILING-INVARIANT: in an unborn-HEAD repo, tool returns a valid empty report and no failure exit. This edge case is defined by real git repo state and real command error semantics (e.g., rev parsing failures), so the boundary is system-level CLI+git interaction. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing repo metadata can hide real git failure paths and exit codes. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/OS repo state is required. REJECTION-GATE: behavior text concerns git state handling only; no model output or agent pipeline, so Agentic/Workflow do not apply.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run CLI via subprocess, assert success exit and structured empty report (all categories zero/empty, per schema).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MIN-FAILING-INVARIANT: for a known commit sequence, reported category totals equal ground truth from actual repository history. Real failures often occur at git revision-range selection, rename detection over history, and subprocess output interpretation; these require real repo history and real git behavior. MOCK-MISS: in-process fake history objects may not reproduce git’s true semantics. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/history behavior is needed. REJECTION-GATE: text describes deterministic history/count correctness, not AI judgment or multi-agent sequencing; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with scripted commits covering add/delete/rename/modify cases. Compute expected totals from the scripted operations (or independent git plumbing commands) and run CLI; assert exact count equality per category.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MIN-FAILING-INVARIANT: given equivalent file changes, report for index (staged) and working tree (unstaged) paths is identical. This depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics and real diff command behavior, which are subprocess/repo-state boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git responses can easily force equivalence and miss real staged/unstaged divergence bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, requires real repo/index/subprocess. REJECTION-GATE: behavior is about git state equivalence only; no AI/ML output dependency or multi-agent chain, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create identical logical change scenarios once staged and once unstaged (on clean baselines), run CLI in both modes, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
